title: Economics
questions: What is economics?
Are you interested in economics?
How important is economics?
Do you think economics is a good subject to study?
What are the biggest economic problems in your country?
Do you think economics is a science?
How is economics different under socialist and capitalist systems?
What was the last thing you read or heard about economics?
Do you think economics is the most important thing people think about in an election?
Do you read or watch economics news in newspapers and on the TV?
What are the biggest economic problems in the world?
Do you know what the difference is between macroeconomics and microeconomics?
What do you know about economic growth?
What do you understand by the term 'environmental economics'?
Which people in this world care most about economics?
What do you think 'happiness economics' is?
Low tax, Laissez-Faire, economies are better than ones with large public sectors. Do you agree?
It is better to be within large economic unions like the European Community rather than outside them. Do you agree?
Governments should legislate to prevent monopolies becoming too powerful. Do you agree?
Free trade policies are always better than protectionist ones. Do you agree?
Governments should not subsidize enterprises which are unprofitable. Do you agree?
Countries should try to become self-sufficient in food and basic necessities. Do you agree?
Giving financial incentives to companies which locate themselves in underdeveloped regions is a waste of public money. It is better to let them set up where they want to be. Do you agree?
Introducing the minimum wage will encourage foreign investors to take their enterprises elsewhere. Do you agree?
